The new Opus 4.7 model places #1 on our Vibe Code Benchmark, at 71%.

When we first released the benchmark 4.5 months ago, no model scored above 25%.

This benchmark tests a model’s ability to create a fully functional web application from the ground up.

Follow for changelogs, API releases, community updates, and deep dives.

New version of Claude Code in the desktop app dropped today with tons of new features and performance improvements. 

Between Cowork and Code, I've found I don't really need to open other apps (or even my terminal) to do most of my work now.

Claude for Word is now in beta.

Draft, edit, and revise documents directly from the sidebar. Claude preserves your formatting, and edits appear as tracked changes.

I've found Managed Agents to somehow be both the fastest way to hack together a weekend agent project and the most robust way to ship one to millions of users.

It eliminates all the complexity of self-hosting an agent but still allows a great degree of flexibility with setting
We released Claude Opus 4.6 just two months ago. Today we're sharing some info on our new model, Claude Mythos Preview.

2 years ago, I started teaching my AI/ML Engineering cohort.

I teach people the way I learned:

1. You show up.
2. You build
3. You struggle
4. You learn
5. You ship

I've read a ton about Brain Computer Interfaces, and while it sounds like science fiction, it actually isn't.

The form factor here seems to be a small chip in a beanie, which is not egregious. I could handle that :)

Now, this is a bold idea!

When your agent gets stuck, it hires a HUMAN to complete a task.

So, imagine a marketplace where people sign up to complete tasks, and it's an AGENT who hires them.

Can't wait to see where this goes.

You need to read more, and you need to consume less. You need to set aside time to do a workout for the mind, and you need to give it time to recover. Especially now that it's so easy for your cognitive capacity to atrophy. Refuse to become mentally obese.

If you want to achieve anything great, it needs to become your one true priority. The only thing on your mind. Nobody accidentally got rich from business. Nobody accidentally built a great physique. They were obsessed with it for multiple years until it became their default.

If you're a writer, be okay with repeating what's already been said, because most ideas have already been exhausted, and genuinely novel ideas are rare.

Nobody follows you because you're talking about something new. They follow you because they want to read your point of view.

I hate conferences so I started my own.

Here's the story...

In 2010 I was sitting at a big circular table feeling nervous.

You know the type — hotel banquet hall, cheesy gold chairs with greyhound bus pattern cushions.

I was twenty-four but could've passed for seventeen,

Wake up. 

Read this quote: 

"Begin at once to live, and count each separate day as a separate life." — Seneca

Ignore it. Open Claude Code.
Huge savings, same team, more profit. Let the compounding begin.

I've been working on the final production-spec XPS 14 since Omacon, and can only echo Prime's review. The battery is a big step up (and there are more efficiencies to come on OLED!), the screen is sublime, the low weight is a delight, and I even got fully used to the keyboard

There are a lot of great Panther Lake laptops shipping now or shortly, but Dell really did an excellent job with the new XPS. Love a good turn-around story! You would never have gotten me onto a machine with a capacitive-touch ESC key (like the 2025)!

GPT 5.4 on the most basic ask against a 40-line bash script: "Figuring out the best way to approach this is the goal! There's just so much to think through, but I'm staying committed to getting it right." SO DRAMATIC!

In 2023, we spent $3,934,099 on AWS + other hosting. In 2026, our hosting + support bill is down to ~$1m/year due to the cloud exit. Even including all the hardware buying, we will already have saved ~$4m by the end of this year. And going forward, it's ~$3m/yr in savings
